summary: Decompiled and typed a substantial portion of the Heal stage module, replacing many grHeal UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations and empty or undocumented stubs with concrete stage initialization, callback setup, ground object creation, animation/JObj reparenting helpers, player/frame lookup logic, and item-spawn behavior. The PR also introduced grheal.static.h containing local stage data, callback tables, lookup tables, strings, and an extern for grHeal_804D6AF0.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/gr/grheal.c from mostly placeholders into typed C for many grHeal functions.

Implemented grHeal_8021EF3C stage initialization: stores Ground_801C49F8 result in grHeal_804D6AF0, adjusts stage_info.unk8C flags, creates stage ground objects for indices 0, 1, and 3, runs ground setup helpers, and sets blast-zone-related state via Ground_801C3980(2.0F * Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset()).

Implemented grHeal_8021EFEC as a reusable ground-object factory using StageCallbacks, Ground_801C14D0, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback assignment, optional callback0 execution, optional proc creation, and an OSReport failure path.
Implemented grHeal_8021EFC0 to initialize the zako generator with grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0).
Added typed callback-style signatures for many stage functions that take HSD_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, Vec3*, s32, u32, or DynamicsDesc* instead of placeholder macros.
Implemented fn_8021F424 to query ground object 1 and return whether gp->gv.unk.xC4 is nonzero.
Implemented grHeal_8021F474 to initialize state for a ground object, register Ground_801C3D44(fn_8021F424, 10.0f, 20.0f), call lb_800115F4, and clear gp->gv.flatzone2.xC4.
Implemented fn_8021F4C0 with byte/bitfield checks against an argument interpreted at offset 0x34 and sets gp->gv.unk.xC4 under specific conditions.

Implemented grHeal_8021F4E8 and grHeal_8021F628 as animation/JObj helper routines that spawn ground objects, assert on null gobj, run grAnime calls, fetch child JOBJs, reparent them, and clean up or adjust ground state.
Implemented grHeal_8021F5DC to clear gp->x10_flags.b2 and start ground animation using gp->map_id.
Implemented grHeal_8021F70C as a character/player lookup routine with a special case mapping character_id 0x13 to 0x12 and an OSReport fallback if the lookup result is -1.
Implemented grHeal_8021F79C to locate a ground JObj, build a BobOmbRain structure, compute a position with lb_8000B1CC, and call it_8026BE84.
Added src/melee/gr/grheal.static.h with static grHeal_803E83B8 lookup data, grHeal_803E8454 StageCallbacks table, grHeal_803E84C4 StageData for /GrHe.dat, and grHeal_803E851C string bytes.
Updated src/melee/gr/grheal.h to include forward declarations and concrete prototypes for the grHeal module.
smart_moves: Centralized stage ground-object setup in grHeal_8021EFEC, mirroring common stage-module patterns: fetch gobj by map id, clear default callbacks, attach GX link, apply callback table entries, and create a proc when callback2 exists.
Reconstructed the StageCallbacks table before or alongside callback implementations, giving the compiler and future readers a stable map from stage object indices to callback functions.
Replaced placeholder signatures in the public header with concrete types, which makes cross-module calls safer and helps future decomp work avoid propagating UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Preserved literal diagnostics and assert evidence, including OSReport strings and __assert line numbers, which are often important for matching and for identifying original source-file strings.

Used existing ground, animation, and baselib helper APIs rather than open-coding behavior: Ground_801C14D0, GObj_SetupGXLink, grAnime_801C8138, grAnime_801C7FF8, GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjReparent, and Ground_801C4A08.
Kept some functions as typed no-op or constant-return callbacks where the evidence likely only supported their signatures and trivial behavior, rather than overfitting speculative logic.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, static callback tables and StageData can drive much of the function typing: callback slots imply HSD_GObj*/Ground_GObj parameters and bool return types.
Ground object creation routines commonly set Ground callback fields x8_callback, xC_callback, x1C_callback and then wire rendering/proc callbacks through GObj_SetupGXLink and HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
Stage initialization often combines stage_info flag setup, spawning several map object indices, and global ground setup calls; preserve the exact ordering because it can affect matching.

When strings are embedded in static data, the same byte array may be used both as a file name/assert source string and as nearby diagnostic text; avoid prematurely splitting or renaming without evidence.

JObj reparenting helpers in stage code often follow a repeatable pattern: create a temporary ground object, start animation, get root JObj, get child JObj, reparent child to a supplied parent, then clear flags or remove the ground object.
Character lookup tables may include special cases for character IDs and sentinel -1 entries; retain exact sentinel behavior and OSReport fallback paths.
Header cleanup is useful decomp progress in itself: concrete prototypes help expose mismatches between stage callback signatures and implementation assumptions.
naming_conventions: Stage module functions retain address-based grHeal_8021xxxx names because no semantic names were established in the PR.
Local helper functions with unknown semantics use fn_8021F424 and fn_8021F4C0, following the project's address-based fn_ convention for non-public or uncertain symbols.
Static data symbols follow address-based grHeal_803Exxxx names, including grHeal_803E83B8, grHeal_803E8454, grHeal_803E84C4, and grHeal_803E851C.
The new static header is named grheal.static.h, matching the module and separating local static data from grheal.h public declarations.
Types used in prototypes prefer project-specific names such as Ground_GObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, DynamicsDesc, Vec3, StageCallbacks, StageData, and BobOmbRain where evidence supports them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit float constants such as 2.0F, 10.0f, 20.0f, and 0.0f in places likely sensitive to matching.
Preserved unsigned suffixes on constants passed to functions or asserts, such as 4U, 2U, 0x10U, 0x1B8U, and 0x201U.
Kept an explicit cast to f32 for animation frame/time arguments in grAnime_801C7FF8 calls.
Used pointer arithmetic and byte extraction in fn_8021F4C0, including reading offset 0x34 and shifting/masking bits, rather than replacing it with a speculative struct field.
Preserved sentinel-loop behavior in grHeal_8021F70C with entry[frame] != -1 and entry[frame] != character_id.
Added PAD_STACK(4) in grHeal_8021F79C, indicating stack layout was adjusted for matching.
Encoded static strings/data byte-for-byte in grheal.static.h instead of relying entirely on C string literals, likely to preserve layout.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but its body was not present in the excerpt, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Check whether grHeal_8021F0D8 and grHeal_8021F180 remain undecompiled placeholders after this PR and whether later PRs completed them.
Look for later cleanup of grheal.static.h, especially the anonymous yaku declaration and extern struct yaku *grHeal_804D6AF0 typing.
Compare grHeal_8021EFEC against equivalent ground-object setup routines in other stage modules for naming and callback-field consistency.
Search later PRs for semantic names for the xC4 flag manipulated by fn_8021F424, grHeal_8021F474, and fn_8021F4C0.
Verify whether the grHeal_803E851C byte array was later split into a source filename string and OSReport format string.
